speaker: HOTSPUR
text: We’ll fight with him tonight.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: It may not be.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: You give him then advantage.

speaker: VERNON
text: Not a whit.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why say you so? Looks he not for supply?

speaker: VERNON
text: So do we.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: His is certain; ours is doubtful.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Good cousin, be advised. Stir not tonight.

speaker: VERNON
text: Do not, my lord.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: You do not counsel well.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: You speak it out of fear and cold heart.

speaker: VERNON
text: Do me no slander, Douglas. By my life

speaker: VERNON
text: (And I dare well maintain it with my life),

speaker: VERNON
text: If well-respected honor bid me on,

speaker: VERNON
text: I hold as little counsel with weak fear

speaker: VERNON
text: As you, my lord, or any Scot that this day lives.

speaker: VERNON
text: Let it be seen tomorrow in the battle

speaker: VERNON
text: Which of us fears.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Yea, or tonight.

speaker: VERNON
text: Content.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Tonight, say I.

speaker: VERNON
text: Come, come, it may not be. I wonder much,

speaker: VERNON
text: Being men of such great leading as you are,

speaker: VERNON
text: That you foresee not what impediments

speaker: VERNON
text: Drag back our expedition. Certain horse

speaker: VERNON
text: Of my cousin Vernon’s are not yet come up.

speaker: VERNON
text: Your uncle Worcester’s horse came but today,

speaker: VERNON
text: And now their pride and mettle is asleep,

speaker: VERNON
text: Their courage with hard labor tame and dull,

speaker: VERNON
text: That not a horse is half the half of himself.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: So are the horses of the enemy

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In general journey-bated and brought low.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The better part of ours are full of rest.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The number of the King exceedeth ours.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For God’s sake, cousin, stay till all come in.

speaker: BLUNT
text: I come with gracious offers from the King,

speaker: BLUNT
text: If you vouchsafe me hearing and respect.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Welcome, Sir Walter Blunt, and would to God

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: You were of our determination.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Some of us love you well, and even those some

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Envy your great deservings and good name

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Because you are not of our quality

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But stand against us like an enemy.

speaker: BLUNT
text: And God defend but still I should stand so,

speaker: BLUNT
text: So long as out of limit and true rule

speaker: BLUNT
text: You stand against anointed majesty.

speaker: BLUNT
text: But to my charge. The King hath sent to know

speaker: BLUNT
text: The nature of your griefs, and whereupon

speaker: BLUNT
text: You conjure from the breast of civil peace

speaker: BLUNT
text: Such bold hostility, teaching his duteous land

speaker: BLUNT
text: Audacious cruelty. If that the King

speaker: BLUNT
text: Have any way your good deserts forgot,

speaker: BLUNT
text: Which he confesseth to be manifold,

speaker: BLUNT
text: He bids you name your griefs, and with all speed

speaker: BLUNT
text: You shall have your desires with interest

speaker: BLUNT
text: And pardon absolute for yourself and these

speaker: BLUNT
text: Herein misled by your suggestion.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The King is kind, and well we know the King

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Knows at what time to promise, when to pay.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My father and my uncle and myself

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Did give him that same royalty he wears,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And when he was not six-and-twenty strong,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Sick in the world’s regard, wretched and low,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A poor unminded outlaw sneaking home,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My father gave him welcome to the shore;

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And when he heard him swear and vow to God

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He came but to be Duke of Lancaster,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To sue his livery, and beg his peace

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: With tears of innocency and terms of zeal,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My father, in kind heart and pity moved,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Swore him assistance and performed it too.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Now when the lords and barons of the realm

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Perceived Northumberland did lean to him,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The more and less came in with cap and knee,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Met him in boroughs, cities, villages,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Attended him on bridges, stood in lanes,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Laid gifts before him, proffered him their oaths,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Gave him their heirs as pages, followed him

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Even at the heels in golden multitudes.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He presently, as greatness knows itself,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Steps me a little higher than his vow

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Made to my father while his blood was poor

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Upon the naked shore at Ravenspurgh,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And now forsooth takes on him to reform

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Some certain edicts and some strait decrees

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That lie too heavy on the commonwealth,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Cries out upon abuses, seems to weep

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Over his country’s wrongs, and by this face,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This seeming brow of justice, did he win

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The hearts of all that he did angle for,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Proceeded further—cut me off the heads

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of all the favorites that the absent king

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In deputation left behind him here

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: When he was personal in the Irish war.

speaker: BLUNT
text: Tut, I came not to hear this.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Then to the point.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In short time after, he deposed the King,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Soon after that deprived him of his life

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And, in the neck of that, tasked the whole state.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To make that worse, suffered his kinsman March

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: (Who is, if every owner were well placed,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Indeed his king) to be engaged in Wales,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: There without ransom to lie forfeited,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Disgraced me in my happy victories,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Sought to entrap me by intelligence,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Rated mine uncle from the council board,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In rage dismissed my father from the court,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Broke oath on oath, committed wrong on wrong,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And in conclusion drove us to seek out

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This head of safety, and withal to pry

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Into his title, the which we find

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Too indirect for long continuance.

speaker: BLUNT
text: Shall I return this answer to the King?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Not so, Sir Walter. We’ll withdraw awhile.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Go to the King, and let there be impawned

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Some surety for a safe return again,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And in the morning early shall mine uncle

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Bring him our purposes. And so farewell.

speaker: BLUNT
text: I would you would accept of grace and love.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And maybe so we shall.

speaker: BLUNT
text: Pray God you do.
